title: MyoUnit Essentials
description: EMG interpretation, calibration, and clinical application using the Myo Unit and MyoLogic Pro, including free-response prompts.

questions.prompt: What is the primary purpose of the Myo Unit system in clinical settings?
options: To evaluate muscle signals, optimize electrode placement, and prepare users for advanced myoelectric control.
To monitor cardiovascular health during physical activity.
To diagnose neuromuscular disorders and provide treatment recommendations.
To train clinicians on general physical therapy techniques.

questions.prompt: What must be done to ensure the Myo Unit connects successfully to the MyoLogic Pro app?
options: Turn off Bluetooth on the device and restart the Myo Unit.
Launch the app and wait for the Myo Unit to connect automatically without powering it on.
Ensure the Myo Unit is powered on and stable, and Bluetooth is enabled on the device running the app.
Ensure the Myo Unit is charging, and the app is running in offline mode.

questions.prompt: Write the name of the app used with the Myo Unit for visualization and setup.
What is the correct procedure for connecting EMG electrodes to the Myo Unit?
options: Place the electrode into the back of the holder.
Connect the electrodes to the charging port of the device.
Place the electrodes on the device's power button.
Attach the electrodes directly to the screen.

questions.prompt: What is one primary benefit of using the Training Games mode for EMG practice?
options: It eliminates the need for professional guidance during EMG training.
It guarantees faster recovery for all users.
It helps users learn consistent and repeatable muscle contractions.
It replaces traditional physical therapy exercises.

questions.prompt: What are the three steps involved in the calibration process for the Myo Unit using the MyoLogic Pro app?
options: Resting, flexing the fingers, and extending the fingers.
Resting, contracting the wrist extensors, and rotating the wrist.
Resting, contracting the wrist flexors, and contracting the wrist extensors.
Resting, contracting the wrist flexors, and holding the wrist in a neutral position.

questions.prompt: Match each aspect of the Myo Unit workflow to its role.

pairs.right: Helps clinicians identify optimal electrode sites and assess signal quality.
Prepares patients for successful myoelectric control.
Streamlines the adjustment of prosthetic settings for optimal performance.

choices: Helps clinicians identify optimal electrode sites and assess signal quality.
Prepares patients for successful myoelectric control.
Streamlines the adjustment of prosthetic settings for optimal performance.

questions.prompt: What is the primary criterion for identifying a reliable EMG site?
options: The site consistently produces strong and repeatable EMG signals.
The site is located on the largest muscle in the region.
The site is located near a major nerve bundle.
The site shows minimal electrical activity at rest.

questions.prompt: How does the Myo Unit integrate with the MyoLogic Pro app to enhance clinical workflows?
options: It replaces the need for real-time EMG signal visualization.
It provides enhanced visualization, calibration simulation, and workflow continuity.
It eliminates the need for patient training sessions.
It allows the Myo Unit to function without EMG electrodes.

questions.prompt: What kind of signals does the Myo Unit visualize in real time?
